evals.prompt: I just cloned this .NET console app repo and I want Copilot to be able to help me with it properly. Can you set up the AI stuff?
evals.expected_output: Detects .NET version from global.json, creates copilot-instructions.md with actual build commands, creates copilot-setup-steps.yml, copies universal skills. Does NOT create platform-specific scoped instructions since this is a simple console app.

evals.prompt: ok so we have this ASP.NET Web API at work with about 200 xUnit tests across 3 test projects, all CI is on GitHub Actions. I'd love for the AI coding agent to actually understand our codebase when it reviews PRs. Can you bootstrap the AI infrastructure?
evals.expected_output: Detects xUnit from NuGet references, creates write-tests-agent configured for xUnit, creates run-tests skill with all 3 test project paths, configures pr-build-status for GitHub Actions with gh run view commands, creates scoped test instructions.

evals.prompt: We have a large cross-platform .NET project with Android, iOS, and Windows targets. Multiple test frameworks — xUnit for unit tests, NUnit for integration tests. CI is on Azure Pipelines. Set up the AI infrastructure.
evals.expected_output: Detects platform-specific code patterns (*.Android.cs, *.iOS.cs), creates platform-scoped instructions for Android and iOS, detects both xUnit and NUnit test projects, creates copilot-setup-steps with workload install, configures pr-build-status for Azure Pipelines with REST API commands.

evals.prompt: We already have a copilot-instructions.md and a couple of skills set up, but I know we're missing a lot. Can you fill in the gaps?
evals.expected_output: Detects existing copilot-instructions.md and skills, asks user before overwriting or merging, only adds missing skills/agents/workflows, does not duplicate what already exists.

evals.prompt: Our CI runs on Azure Pipelines and we want the AI to be able to read build failures when it's working on PRs. Set up the full AI dev environment.
evals.expected_output: Detects azure-pipelines.yml or eng/pipelines/, configures pr-build-status with Azure Pipelines REST API curl commands, still creates copilot-setup-steps.yml (GitHub-side), notes AZDO_PAT requirement in copilot-instructions.md.
